_description: Structured database of abstraction failure and success patterns. Add entries as experiments are completed. See project_spec.md §10.4 for schema and §12 for taxonomy framework.

patterns.name: Launch Overhead Dominance
patterns.symptom: Abstraction 30-60% slower than native for kernels with execution time < 50 µs
patterns.root_cause_detail: Extra function call layers not fully inlined; runtime dispatch overhead accumulates when kernel execution time is short
affected_workloads: irregular applications with many small launches
mitigation: Coarsen task granularity to amortize launch cost

evidence.note: Julia small (N=32³) eff=0.70 and medium (N=128³) eff=0.69 are consistent with @cuda launch overhead (~0.2–0.5ms) dominating short kernel times (N=32 kernel time ≈0.014ms, N=128 ≈0.16ms). At large N (N=256³, kernel time ≈2.7ms) Julia large eff=1.376 — but this is a thermal artifact (native large session was hotter than Julia large session). Native CUDA overhead (no JIT, no Julia runtime) shows no corresponding degradation at small N. RAJA small eff=0.94 confirms the penalty is Julia-specific, not kernel-size-specific for all abstractions.

patterns.name: Compiler Backend Unrolling Failure
patterns.symptom: Abstraction 2-4x slower on NVIDIA for nested loops; matches on AMD
patterns.root_cause_detail: NVCC/PTX backend fails to unroll template-instantiated loops correctly; AMD LLVM backend handles the same patterns without regression
mitigation: Use vendor-tuned library (cuBLAS) for the failing kernel
File compiler bug report with minimal reproducer

evidence.note: Benchmark eff=0.24 is thermally contaminated (sequential session: raja_naive runs after ~130s of native+cublas load; 2.76x thermal ratio confirmed by fresh-state nsys profiling at 116 GFLOP/s vs benchmark 42 GFLOP/s). Thermal-corrected eff=0.66 (116/175.1) is the representative value. True root cause is P004 API Limitation (RAJA forall cannot express shared-memory tiling); P002 Compiler Backend Failure not confirmed — cudaLaunchKernel overhead is 0.05% of runtime. Full compiler backend validation (P002) pending ncu hardware counters on A100 (RmProfilingAdminOnly=1 blocks hardware PMU on RTX 5060).

patterns.literature: Godoy et al. 2023 — Julia DGEMM on A100

patterns.name: Memory Layout Mismatch
patterns.symptom: 20-40% bandwidth reduction vs. native; L2 hit rate drops from ~75% to ~45%
patterns.root_cause_detail: Abstraction defaults to non-optimal array layout (e.g., LayoutRight when LayoutLeft is required for NVIDIA coalescing); causes strided instead of unit-stride access
affected_workloads: multi-dimensional arrays with high data reuse
mitigation: Explicitly specify Kokkos::LayoutLeft for NVIDIA kernels
Profile L2 hit rate before and after layout change
Document layout requirements per platform in config.yaml

evidence.note: Kokkos small eff=0.76. RAJA small eff=0.94 with identical kernel logic but direct block/thread mapping — P003 layout mismatch NOT confirmed. Kokkos uses MDRangePolicy<Rank<3>> with tiling hint {2,4,32}; tiling overhead (block scheduling, boundary handling) is more likely than layout mismatch. Kokkos large eff=0.703 is thermally biased (native large session ran in hotter GPU state). P003 remains unconfirmed in E3 — see P006 (Tiling Policy Overhead) as the more likely explanation.

success_patterns.name: Layout-Induced Coalescing Advantage
success_patterns.symptom: Abstraction efficiency > 1.0 vs cuBLAS ceiling reference
success_patterns.root_cause: Column-major Fortran layout in Julia eliminates transposition overhead; warp-level memory access patterns are superior to row-major C implementations at all compute-bound problem sizes
success_patterns.root_cause_detail: Julia column-major CuArrays map warp threads (varying threadIdx.x → row) to consecutive elements of the same matrix column, giving perfect coalescing on A and broadcast access on B. The native tiled C kernel uses row-major storage and incurs 512 __syncthreads() barriers at N=8192 (256 tiles × 2 barriers). At arithmetic intensity ≈ 683 FLOP/byte (compute-bound), synchronisation overhead exceeds shared-memory bandwidth savings. Fresh-state nsys profiling confirms the advantage is size-independent: eff≈1.29 at N=4096 (medium) and eff≈1.20–1.25 at N=8192 (large). The apparent non-monotonic benchmark scaling (0.80 medium → 1.25 large) was a thermal contamination artifact, not a size threshold.

evidence.validation_method: CUDA event timing (CUDA.@elapsed) confirms GPU-only kernel time produces same GFLOP/s as wall-clock measurement; max_rel_err < 1e-6 at N=128. Fresh-state nsys profiling (profiles/e2/julia_naive_medium_nsys.nsys-rep) confirms medium-N advantage: 225 GFLOP/s fresh vs 139 GFLOP/s benchmark (1.62x thermal ratio). Large-N benchmark/profiling delta is only 4%, confirming large-N result is thermally clean.
evidence.kernel_storage_order_confirmed: nsys kernel name contains (Int)1 storage-order parameter, confirming column-major layout

success_patterns.implication: Layout conventions can dominate algorithmic overhead at large problem sizes. Expert optimisation assumptions (tiling always improves performance) do not hold in compute-bound regimes where synchronisation overhead dominates. Decision framework must condition tiling recommendation on measured arithmetic intensity.
success_patterns.mitigation: N/A — this is a performance advantage for the abstraction. For the native kernel: use cuBLAS or profile-guided tiling decisions conditioned on N.
success_patterns.ppc_impact: PPC > 1.0 — abstraction exceeds native baseline; counts as excellent portability but native baseline is not the theoretical ceiling

success_patterns.name: Load Imbalance Amplification
success_patterns.symptom: Abstraction efficiency collapses multiplicatively below what P001 Launch Overhead predicts alone — effect is multiplicative, not additive
success_patterns.root_cause_detail: Power-law degree distribution (Pareto γ=2.5) creates extreme row-length variance. One-thread-per-row mapping leaves most warps stalled waiting on the few long-row threads (warp divergence + thread idling). For interpreted runtimes (Julia @cuda), this compounds with P001 Launch Overhead: P001 alone predicts eff≈0.56 for julia/small, but power_law pushes it to 0.45 — a 0.11 multiplicative penalty on top of the 0.44 already from P001. The effect shrinks at larger N as tail rows dominate a smaller fraction of total runtime.
mitigation: Warp-per-row CSR (multiple threads collaborate on one row, reduces idle warps)
CSR segmented reduction (CUB DeviceSegmentedReduce) for load-balanced SpMV
Row reordering to group similar-length rows in the same warp
Use native CUDA for power-law workloads at small N; abstraction safe at large N where collapse is uniform

evidence.note: Distinguishing feature vs P001: julia/laplacian/small eff=0.56 is P001 alone (kernel ~0.05ms). julia/power_law/small eff=0.45 — same launch overhead, but power-law nnz variance (max degree ~256, min=1) means most threads idle, amplifying the launch-overhead fraction. Multiplicative compound: P001 factor × P007 factor, not P001 + P007 additive sum. Effect size decays with N: julia/power_law/large eff=0.91 (P007 amortized, P001 also amortized). RAJA is immune at large N (eff=0.99) — direct forall without block scheduling overhead has no secondary amplification.

success_patterns.ppc_impact: 0.40-0.70 at small N with power-law inputs; near-native at large N (P007 amortizes)
success_patterns.literature: Merrill et al. 2015 (merge-based SpMV), Yang et al. 2018 (design principles for sparse GPU kernels)

e4_experiments.note: AI≈0.13 FLOP/byte — firmly memory-bound. power_law matrix creates extreme load imbalance (Pareto gamma=2.5). Numba UNSUPPORTED_CC120 expected (same PTX 9.2 issue as E2/E3). No warp-per-row optimization — honest one-thread-per-row baseline. Expected hypothesis: memory-bound AI masks abstraction overhead for laplacian_2d and random_sparse; power_law may expose P001 Launch Overhead or reveal new load-imbalance pattern.

e5_experiments.metric: GFLOP/s = 2*nnz / time_s / 1e9 (consistent with E4; SpTRSV is latency-bound not bandwidth-bound)

n_levels_expected.lower_triangular_laplacian: O(sqrt(N)) — regular BFS wavefronts from 2D grid
n_levels_expected.lower_triangular_random: O(N) worst case; typically O(log N) to O(N^0.5) depending on graph diameter

skipped.numba: UNSUPPORTED_CC120 — Numba 0.64.0 / Blackwell CC 12.0 PTX mismatch (same as E2-E4)
skipped.sycl: NO_COMPILER — no SYCL compiler on nvidia_rtx5060_laptop

e5_experiments.kernel_design: One CUDA/Kokkos/RAJA/Julia kernel launch per level; one thread per row within each level. x reset (cudaMemset) excluded from timed region. Level-set constructed CPU-side (BFS); n_levels kernel launches per solve.
e5_experiments.primary_hypothesis: SpTRSV efficiency gap = P001 Launch Overhead × n_levels. Each abstraction pays its per-launch overhead n_levels times per solve (not once). Julia: ~0.3ms × n_levels; Kokkos: Kokkos::fence overhead × n_levels; RAJA: RAJA::synchronize × n_levels. Compounds with P007 Load Imbalance for lower_triangular_random (irregular level widths). Expected: laplacian (regular levels) → efficiency decays predictably with n_levels; random (irregular) → additional efficiency penalty from uneven warps.
e5_experiments.expected_new_pattern: P008 candidate: Level-Set Dispatch Amplification — abstraction per-launch overhead multiplied by n_levels, creating O(n_levels) overhead accumulation. Distinct from P001 (single-kernel overhead) in that the penalty scales with problem structure (n_levels), not just problem size. Testable: plot efficiency vs n_levels across matrix types and sizes.
e5_experiments.csv_columns: timestamp, experiment_id, kernel, abstraction, platform, matrix_type, problem_size, n_rows, nnz, n_levels, max_level_width, min_level_width, run_id, execution_time_ms, throughput_gflops, hw_state_verified

p008_evidence.hypothesis: Level-Set Dispatch Amplification: efficiency gap = f(n_levels) not f(N). Julia laplacian eff degrades from 0.75 (n_levels=31) to 0.46 (n_levels=181) as n_levels grows — NOT a problem-size effect. Random matrix at equivalent N has fewer levels (75 vs 181) and julia eff=0.90 vs 0.46. Confirms the penalty is keyed to n_levels, not N.
p008_evidence.raja_random_large_eff_1.30: RAJA forall over wide levels (max_lw=524) — wide levels mean each RAJA launch covers many rows, amortising per-launch overhead. Same Kokkos mechanism at eff=1.11. Both exceed native because wide parallel wavefronts fill the GPU better than native's sequential-through-small-kernels baseline.
p008_evidence.kokkos_random_large_eff_1.11: Same mechanism as RAJA. Kokkos RangePolicy dispatches the same kernel structure but with slightly more overhead per launch than RAJA forall → lower eff than RAJA.
p008_evidence.best_abstraction: RAJA: best across all configs (only abstraction that degrades gracefully with n_levels AND gains efficiency on wide-level matrices)
p008_evidence.worst_abstraction: Julia: eff=0.46 at laplacian/large (n_levels=181) — @cuda dispatch overhead × 181 levels dominates compute time completely

e6_experiments.metric: GTEPS = n_edges / time_s / 1e9 (stored as throughput_gflops for CSV schema consistency)

skipped.numba: UNSUPPORTED_CC120 — same Numba 0.64.0/Blackwell CC 12.0 PTX mismatch as E2-E5
skipped.sycl: NO_COMPILER — no SYCL compiler on nvidia_rtx5060_laptop

e6_experiments.kernel_design: Two-phase per BFS level: scatter (one thread per frontier vertex, atomicCAS on d_distances) + compact (Thrust copy_if for native; parallel_scan for Kokkos; exclusive_scan + gather for RAJA; CUDA.cumsum for Julia). One kernel launch per phase per level. d_distances reset excluded from timed region.
e6_experiments.primary_hypothesis: BFS efficiency gap is driven by two independent sources: (1) P008 Level-Set Dispatch Amplification — n_levels kernel launches × per-launch overhead (same as E5 SpTRSV). (2) Frontier irregularity — irregular frontier widths create load imbalance within the scatter phase (compound with P007). Erdos-Renyi (irregular frontiers) expected to show larger abstraction gap than 2d_grid (regular diamond frontiers).
e6_experiments.p006_note: P006 (Tiling Policy Overhead) is NOT expected in BFS. BFS uses flat RangePolicy/forall over the frontier — no multi-dimensional tiling. P006 absence in E6 would confirm P006 is specific to tiled kernels (E2 DGEMM, E3 Stencil).
e6_experiments.expected_new_pattern: None beyond P007 + P008 compounds. If Kokkos/RAJA exceed native at large N with wide frontiers (similar to E5 random/large result), that would extend the wide-parallelism advantage pattern established in E5.
e6_experiments.csv_columns: timestamp, experiment_id, kernel, abstraction, platform, graph_type, problem_size, n_vertices, n_edges, n_levels, max_frontier_width, min_frontier_width, peak_frontier_fraction, run_id, execution_time_ms, throughput_gflops, hw_state_verified
e6_experiments.profile_csv: data/raw/bfs_profile_{platform}_{date}.csv — per-level frontier widths (for fig24 and frontier_irregularity computation)

key_results.p008_evidence: P008 (Level-Set Dispatch Amplification) confirmed: julia 2d_grid eff collapses from 0.352 (n_levels=63) to 0.439 (n_levels=255) to 0.358 (n_levels=511). erdos_renyi julia eff stays 0.43-0.73 with n_levels=6-8 (shallow DAG). Confirming P008: 2d_grid (deep, narrow) is WORSE than erdos_renyi (shallow, wide) for julia — n_levels is the dominant predictor, not irregularity.
key_results.irregularity_verdict: Frontier irregularity does NOT predict lower efficiency. 2d_grid (low irregularity=0.57, regular wavefront) has WORSE julia efficiency (0.35-0.44) than erdos_renyi (high irregularity=1.25-1.64, eff=0.43-0.73). The dominant factor is n_levels (P008), not frontier width variance.
key_results.kokkos_raja_absent: Kokkos and RAJA not available (libraries not installed). Only 2 abstractions measured: native and julia.

_notes: All patterns marked 'hypothesis' must be validated with experiment evidence before paper submission.
Add 'evidence' array entries referencing experiment_id values from data/performance.csv.
Change status from 'hypothesis' to 'validated' once experiment_ids are added.

P002 is 'partially_validated': raja_naive/medium benchmark eff=0.24 is thermally contaminated (2.76x thermal ratio; see profiles/e2/e2_profiling_notes.md). Thermal-corrected eff=0.66. True root cause is P004 API Limitation (RAJA forall cannot express tiling), not P002 Compiler Backend Failure. P002 classification pending ncu hardware counters on A100.

P005 is 'validated': julia_naive/large efficiency=1.25 confirmed by GPU event timing and correctness check. Deep profiling (2026-03-15) further confirms Pattern 5 is size-independent: fresh-state eff≈1.29 at N=4096, eff≈1.20 at N=8192. Medium-N benchmark eff=0.80 was a thermal artifact, not a size threshold for the pattern.

Thermal Contamination Pattern (informally P004_Thermal): sequential benchmark sessions create differential thermal loading when kernel durations vary across abstractions. Warmup-50 is insufficient — must use max(50 iterations, 60s) for variable-duration kernels. This affects E2 medium-N results for both raja_naive and julia_naive.

P006 candidate: Tiling Policy Overhead at Small Granularity — Kokkos MDRangePolicy with large tiling hints {2,4,32} shows eff=0.76 at N=32³ vs RAJA eff=0.94. At very small grids, tile boundaries dominate active work cells, and block scheduling overhead amortizes poorly. Validated direction: E3 evidence. Needs ncu occupancy analysis at N=32 to confirm.

E3 hypothesis verdict: memory-bound stencil → all abstractions near-native PARTIALLY confirmed. RAJA at medium eff=0.99 confirms hypothesis. Kokkos medium eff=0.91 supports it. Thermal instability prevents clean verification at N=256 without locked clocks. Julia medium eff=0.69 confirms P001 Launch Overhead Dominance (now validated). P003 Memory Layout Mismatch NOT confirmed in E3 — RAJA vs Kokkos divergence points to tiling overhead (P006), not layout.

E7 N-Body (2026-03-16, COMPLETE): FCC lattice LJ force (r_cut=2.5σ, ρ=0.8442). N=4000/32000/256000. n_nbrs_mean=54.0, n_nbrs_max=54, AI=0.971 FLOP/byte (memory-bound). Notile (neighbor-list): native 207/373/180 GFLOP/s; julia 64/189/170 GFLOP/s; julia eff=0.31/0.51/0.95. Julia small/medium flagged (P001 launch overhead dominant at short kernel durations). Julia large eff=0.95 (good tier): kernel duration ~4ms hides launch overhead. P006 CONFIRMED via tile kernel: all-pairs tile speedup 4x/7x/13x over notile (small/medium/large). Shared-memory cooperative warp loading eliminates repeated global-memory reads for large N. Kokkos and RAJA not installed. VRAM at N=256K: 134.4 MB used.
